source_idiom: sur le bout de la langue
source_meaning: on the tip of one's tongue (known but not quite remembered)
target_meaning: to be at the back of one's mind; to keep coming to mind

source_meaning: very rarely or never; once in a blue moon
target_meaning: adverbial form of 시(時)도 때도 없다 (sido ttaedo eopda, “to be recurrent”): recurrently

source_meaning: to make someone's blood run cold, to make someone's blood curdle
to pass out, to black out, to keel over, to faint
target_meaning: to fall asleep, go to sleep, nod off

source_meaning: to be worried sick, to be worried to death
to get off someone's back, to leave someone alone
target_meaning: a psychological burden that is relieved
